metadata.summary: Linked Data Platform interface for Django Rest Framework

functions.name: djangoldp.utils.check_client_ip
documentation: this method is used to check if a given IP is part of the PASSTHROUGH_IPS list

functions.name: djangoldp.related.get_prefetch_fields
documentation:
This method should then be used with queryset.prefetch_related, to auto-fetch joined resources (to speed up nested serialization)
This can speed up ModelViewSet and LDPViewSet alike by as high a factor as 2
\:param model: the model to be analysed
\:param serializer: an LDPSerializer instance. Used to extract the fields for each nested model
\:param depth: the depth at which to stop the recursion (should be set to the configured depth of the ViewSet)
\:param prepend_string: should be set to the default. Used in recursive calls
\:return: set of strings to prefetch for a given model. Including serialized nested fields and foreign keys recursively
called on many-to-many fields until configured depth reached

methods.name: djangoldp.conf.ldpsettings.LDPSettings.__init__
documentation: Build a Django Setting object from a dict.

methods.name: djangoldp.conf.ldpsettings.LDPSettings.build_settings
documentation:
Look for the parameters in multiple places.
Each step overrides the value of the previous key found. Except for "extend" list. Those value must be lists and all values found are added to these lists without managing duplications.

Resolution order of the configuration:
  1. Core default settings
  2. Packages settings
  3. Code from a local settings.py file
  4. YAML config file

documentation: a version of get_or_create_nested_backlinks in which all nested backlinks are created, or none of them are

methods.name: djangoldp.views.InboxView._get_or_create_nested_backlinks
documentation:
recursively deconstructs a tree of nested objects, using get_or_create on each leaf/branch
\:param obj: Dict representation of the object
\:param object_model: The Model class of the object. Will be discovered if set to None
\:param update: if True will update retrieved objects with new data
\:raises Exception: if get_or_create fails on a branch, the creation will be reversed and the Exception re-thrown

Indicates that the actor has added the object to the target

methods.name: djangoldp.views.InboxView.handle_remove_activity
documentation:
handles Remove Activities. See https://www.w3.org/ns/activitystreams

methods.name: djangoldp.views.InboxView.handle_create_or_update_activity
documentation: handles Create & Update Activities. See https://www.w3.org/ns/activitystreams

methods.name: djangoldp.views.InboxView.handle_delete_activity
documentation:
handles Remove Activities. See https://www.w3.org/ns/activitystreams

methods.name: djangoldp.views.InboxView.handle_follow_activity
documentation:
handles Follow Activities. See https://www.w3.org/ns/activitystreams
Indicates that the actor is following the object, and should receive Updates on what happens to it

documentation: returns True if the all the permissions are included in the permissions of the class

documentation: returns the list of methods allowed for the permissions of the class, depending on the permission map

documentation: checks if the request is allowed at all, based on its method and the permissions of the class

documentation: checks if the access to the object is allowed,

methods.name: djangoldp.permissions.LDPBasePermission.get_permissions
documentation: returns the permissions the user has on a given model or on a given object

A base class from which all permission classes should inherit.
Extends the DRF permissions class to include the concept of model-permissions, separate from the view, and to
change to a system of outputting permissions sets for the serialization of WebACLs

methods.name: djangoldp.models.Model.get_container_path
documentation: returns the url path which is used to access actions on this model (e.g. /users/)

methods.name: djangoldp.models.Model.resolve_id
documentation:
Resolves the id of a given path (e.g. /container/1/)
Raises Resolver404 if the path cannot be found, ValidationError if the path is for a model base
and an ObjectDoesNotExist exception if the resource does not exist

methods.name: djangoldp.models.Model.resolve_container
documentation: retruns the model container of passed URL path

methods.name: djangoldp.models.Model.resolve
documentation:
resolves the containing model and associated id in the path. If there is no id in the path returns None
\:param path: a URL path to check
\:return: the container model and resolved id in a tuple

methods.name: djangoldp.models.Model.get_or_create
documentation:
gets an object with the passed urlid if it exists, creates it if not
\:param model: the model class which the object belongs to
\:param update: if set to True the object will be updated with the passed field_tuples
\:param field_tuples: kwargs for the model creation/updating
\:return: the object, fetched or created
\:raises Exception: if the object does not exist, but the data passed is invalid

methods.name: djangoldp.models.Model.get_or_create_external
documentation:
checks that the parameterised urlid is external and then returns the result of Model.get_or_create
\:raises ObjectDoesNotExist: if the urlid is not external and the object doesn't exist

methods.name: djangoldp.models.Model.get_subclass_with_rdf_type
documentation: returns Model subclass with Meta.rdf_type matching parameterised type, or None

methods.name: djangoldp.models.Model.is_external
documentation:
---
param value: string urlid or an instance with urlid field
return: True if the urlid is external to the server, False otherwise
---

classes.name: djangoldp.models.DynamicNestedField
documentation:
Used to define a method as a nested_field.
Usage:
    LDPUser.circles = lambda self: Circle.objects.filter(members__user=self)
    LDPUser.circles.field = DynamicNestedField(Circle, 'circles')

modules.name: djangoldp.management.commands.check_integrity
documentation:
DjangoLDP Check Integrity Command & Importer
Usage `./manage.py check_integrity --help`

This command does import every `check_integrity.py` of every DJANGOLDP_PACKAGES
Allowed methods:
  `add_arguments(parser)`: Allows to create new arguments to this command
  `check_integrity(options)`: Do your own checks on the integrity

Examples on `djangoldp/check_integrity.py`

methods.name: djangoldp.serializers.LDListMixin.check_cache
documentation: Auxiliary function to avoid code duplication - checks cache and returns from it if it has entry

methods.name: djangoldp.serializers.LDListMixin.to_representation
documentation:
Converts internal representation to primitive data representation
Filters objects out which I don't have permission to view
Permission on container :
 - Can Add if add permission on contained object's type
 - Can view the container is view permission on container model : container obj are filtered by view permission
A Mixin for serializing containers into JSONLD format

methods.name: djangoldp.serializers.IdentityFieldMixin.to_internal_value
documentation: Gives the @id as a representation if present

methods.name: djangoldp.serializers.JsonLdField.get_url
documentation: Overridden from DRF to shortcut on urlid-holding objects

classes.name: djangoldp.serializers.JsonLdIdentityField

methods.name: djangoldp.serializers.JsonLdIdentityField.to_representation
documentation: returns hyperlink representation of identity field
Represents an identity (url) field for a serializer

methods.name: djangoldp.serializers.LDPSerializer.__repr__
documentation: The default serializer repr ends in infinite loop. Overloading it prevents that.

methods.name: djangoldp.serializers.LDPSerializer.handle_value_object
documentation:
In JSON-LD value-objects can be passed in, which store some context on the field passed. By overriding this
function you can react to this context on a field without overriding build_standard_field

methods.name: djangoldp.serializers.LDPSerializer.attach_related_object
documentation: adds m2m relations included in validated_data to the instance

methods.name: djangoldp.serializers.LDPSerializer.remove_empty_value
documentation: sets any empty strings in the validated_data to None

methods.name: djangoldp.serializers.LDPSerializer.resolve_fk_instances
documentation:
iterates over every dict object in validated_data and resolves them into instances (get or create)
\:param model: the model being operated on
\:param validated_data: the data passed to the serializer
\:param create: set to True, foreign keys will be created if they do not exist

methods.name: djangoldp.management.commands.configure.Command.add_arguments
documentation: Define the same arguments as the ones in CLI.

methods.name: djangoldp.management.commands.configure.Command.handle
documentation: Wrapper command around default django initialization commands.

classes.name: djangoldp.filters.OwnerFilterBackend
documentation: Adds the objects owned by the user

classes.name: djangoldp.filters.PublicFilterBackend
documentation:
Public filter applied.
This class can be applied on models which bears a is_public boolean field, to filter objects that are public.

classes.name: djangoldp.filters.ActiveFilterBackend
documentation: Filter which removes inactive objects from the queryset, useful for user for instance and configurable using the active_field meta

classes.name: djangoldp.filters.NoFilterBackend
documentation:
No filter applied.
This class is useful for permission classes that don't filter objects, so that they can be chained with other

classes.name: djangoldp.filters.LocalObjectFilterBackend
documentation:
Filter which removes external objects (federated backlinks) from the queryset
For querysets which should only include local objects

classes.name: djangoldp.filters.LocalObjectOnContainerPathBackend
documentation:
Override of LocalObjectFilterBackend which removes external objects when the view requested
is the model container path

classes.name: djangoldp.filters.SearchByQueryParamFilterBackend
documentation: Applies search fields in the request query params to the queryset

classes.name: djangoldp.middleware.AllowRequestedCORSMiddleware
documentation: A CORS Middleware which allows the domains requested by the request

classes.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityInMemoryCache
djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService.revive_activities
documentation: re-schedules all ScheduledActivities to the queue

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService.start
documentation:
method checks if there are scheduled activities on the queue and starts them up
Important: this method should only be called in start-up, when you know there are not queue tasks running
otherwise duplicate activities may be sent

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService.do_post
documentation:
makes a POST request to url, passing activity
:returns: response from server
:raises: Timeout or ConnectionError if the post could not be made

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService._save_activity_from_response
documentation:
wrapper to save a finished Activity based on the parameterised response
\:return: saved Activity object

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService._attempt_failed_reschedule
documentation:
either re-schedules a failed activity or saves its failure state, depending on the number of fails and the
fail policy (MAX_ACTIVITY_RESCHEDULES)
\:return: True if it was able to reschedule

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService._send_activity
documentation:
makes a POST request to url, passing ScheduledActivity instance. reschedules if needed
\:param backoff_factor: a factor to use in the extension of waiting for retries. Used both in the RetryStrategy
of requests.post and in rescheduling an activity which timed out

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService._activity_queue_worker
documentation:
Worker for sending a scheduled activity on the queue. Decides whether to send the activity and then passes to
_send_activity if it is worth it

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService._update_is_new
documentation: auxiliary function which validates if a scheduled update holds new information, compared to a past success

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService._add_remove_is_new
documentation: auxiliary function validates if the receiver does not know about this Add/Remove activity

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService._push_to_queue
documentation: wrapper to check for singleton initialization before pushing

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService.resend_activity
documentation:
a variation of send_activity for ScheduledActivity objects
\:param url: the recipient url inbox
\:param scheduled_activity: a ScheduledActivity object for sending
\:param failed: set to True to increment scheduled_activity.failed_attempts, to keep track of the number of resends

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService.send_activity
documentation:
saves a ScheduledActivity for the parameterised activity and passes it to the queue
\:param url: the recipient url inbox
\:param activity: an Activity to send

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityQueueService._save_sent_activity
documentation:
Auxiliary function saves a record of parameterised activity
\:param model_represenation: the model class which should be used to store the activity. Defaults to djangol.Activity, must be a subclass
Manages an asynchronous queue for Activity format messages

classes.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.build_object_tree
documentation: builds a depth 1 object tree from a parameterised instance, with each branch being an object's urlid and RDF type

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.get_actor_from_user_instance
documentation: Auxiliary function returns valid Actor object from parameterised user instance, None if parameter invalid

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.discover_inbox
documentation: a method which discovers the inbox of the target resource

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.build_activity
documentation: Auxiliary function returns an activity object with kwargs in the body

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.send_add_activity
documentation:
Sends an Add activity
\:param actor: a valid Actor object
\:param obj: a valid ActivityStreams Object
\:param target: an object representing the target collection

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.send_remove_activity
documentation:
Sends a Remove activity
\:param actor: a valid Actor object, or a user instance
\:param obj: a valid ActivityStreams Object
\:param origin: the context the object has been removed from

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.send_create_activity
documentation:
Sends a Create activity
\:param actor: a valid Actor object, or a user instance
\:param obj: a valid ActivityStreams Object
\:param inbox: the inbox to send the activity to

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.send_update_activity
documentation:
Sends an Update activity
\:param actor: a valid Actor object, or a user instance
\:param obj: a valid ActivityStreams Object
\:param inbox: the inbox to send the activity to

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.send_delete_activity
documentation:
Sends a Remove activity
\:param actor: a valid Actor object, or a user instance
\:param obj: a valid ActivityStreams Object
\:param inbox: the inbox to send the activity to

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.get_related_externals
documentation: Auxiliary function returns a set of urlids of distant resources connected to paramertised instance

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.get_target_inboxes
documentation: Auxiliary function returns a set of inboxes, from a set of target object urlids

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.get_follower_inboxes
documentation: Auxiliary function returns a set of inboxes, from the followers of parameterised object urlid

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.save_followers_for_targets
documentation:
saves Follower objects for any external urlid which isn't already following the object in question
\:param external_urlids: list of external urlids to populate the follower inbox
\:param obj_id: object id to be followed

methods.name: djangoldp.activities.services.ActivityPubService.remove_followers_for_resource
documentation: removes all followers which match the follower urlid, obj urlid combination
A service aiding the construction and sending of ActivityStreams notifications
